name: R-73 (Vympel)
description: Russian short-range IR dogfight missile (NATO: AA-11 Archer). The missile that, paired with helmet-mounted sights on late-Soviet MiG-29s, gave Russian fighters a decisive WVR off-boresight advantage through the 1990s — pre-AIM-9X this was the best dogfight missile in the world. Cooled IR seeker with ±60° off-boresight cuing, thrust-vectoring tail vanes for high-G turns post-launch. Less flare-resistant than IIR seekers (AIM-9X) but a much harder target than AIM-9M to spoof. Carried by every Russian/Soviet-derived fighter from MiG-29 onward.
